CS3.301.name: Operating Systems
CS3.301.description: Introduces the design and implementation of modern operating systems through xv6. Covers processes, threads, scheduling, virtual memory, file systems, and concurrency. Heavy lab component — expect to read and write substantial C kernel code across five projects culminating in a custom file system.

CS7.401.name: Introduction to NLP
CS7.401.description: Foundations of natural language processing: tokenization, language models, sequence labeling, parsing, semantics, and modern neural approaches. Project-driven with weekly programming assignments in PyTorch.

CS5.604.name: Computer Vision
CS5.604.description: Classical and deep learning approaches to computer vision: image formation, filtering, feature detection, stereo, segmentation, and convolutional networks. Strong project component with applications to real datasets.

CS1.201.name: Data Structures
CS1.201.description: Linear and non-linear data structures, asymptotic analysis, hashing, balanced trees, and graph algorithms. Implementations in C++. Foundational course for all CSE students.

EC2.202.name: Digital Systems & Microcontrollers
EC2.202.description: Combinational and sequential digital logic, finite state machines, microcontroller architecture, and embedded systems lab work on ARM Cortex-M. Heavy lab component with weekly hardware assignments.

HS2.103.name: History & Philosophy of Science
HS2.103.description: Surveys major epistemological shifts from Aristotle to Kuhn. Covers scientific revolutions, the demarcation problem, and philosophy of physics and biology. Lighter reading load than most HS electives but heavy discussion participation.

MA3.102.name: Probability & Random Processes
MA3.102.description: Probability spaces, random variables, expectation, common distributions, convergence, and an introduction to Markov chains and Poisson processes. Requires comfort with real analysis and linear algebra.

CS6.301.name: Distributed Systems
CS6.301.description: Consensus, replication, time, transactions, and modern distributed storage. Reading-heavy with a substantial systems project in Go. Covers Raft, Paxos, Dynamo, Spanner, and Kafka.

CS4.301.name: Compilers
CS4.301.description: Lexing, parsing, semantic analysis, intermediate representations, optimization, and code generation. Builds a working compiler for a Pascal-like language across four projects. Expect substantial C++ code.

OP3.102.name: Cognitive Science
OP3.102.description: Survey of cognitive science across psychology, linguistics, AI, and philosophy. Strong reading load covering classic experiments in attention, memory, and language. Light assignments but requires consistent engagement.

BQ5.102.name: Game Theory & Mechanism Design
BQ5.102.description: Nash equilibria, auctions, mechanism design, and applications to algorithmic economics. Mathematically rigorous — proofs are expected in homework. Background in discrete math and probability helps.
